prompt:
Goal: Create a minimalist editorial menswear fashion poster for atelier form, with a cinematic studio-photography look and refined magazine typography.

Canvas: Square 1:1 poster, muted neutral palette, soft beige-gray studio background, high-end fashion campaign composition.

Main image: A young male model stands centered, cropped from upper chest to top of hair, facing forward. He has dark curly hair and wears layered modern basics: a dark brown crewneck shirt with a chest pocket, a light taupe overshirt worn open, and a thin silver chain necklace. His face is intentionally covered by a large centered square color block in a warm brown/taupe tone, creating an anonymous editorial identity effect.

Foreground depth: Add a blurred out-of-focus shoulder and back-of-head silhouette along the left edge and lower-left foreground, as if another person or mirror reflection is partially blocking the camera. Keep the foreground soft and dark, adding depth without distracting from the model.

Layout and text content: Use exactly 8 visible text elements. 1) Top-left small brand text: “atelier form”. 2) Top-right small category text: “Editorial / New Season”. 3) Large lowercase headline in the lower-left center: modern essentials, split across two lines with wide letter spacing. 4) Small subtitle under the headline: Studio Collection — Chapter Two. 5) Bottom-left copyright line: “© 2026 | All Rights Reserved”. 6) Right side vertical date near upper-middle: 24.06.2026. 7) Right side vertical label near mid-lower: “Curated Series”. 8) Bottom-right small bookmark icon followed by “Save this look”. Also add a small vertical “Edition 02” near the lower-right edge.

Visual style: Photorealistic fashion editorial, soft diffused lighting, shallow depth of field, muted taupe/brown/gray color grading, clean Swiss-inspired typography, generous negative space, elegant lowercase sans-serif type, premium clothing catalog aesthetic.

Constraints: Keep the face fully hidden by the square block, no visible facial features, no extra logos or watermarks, no extra text beyond the specified poster copy, maintain a polished luxury streetwear campaign feel.
